addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	---
2	name: cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash
3	description: zopfli.c functions that call malloc() directly in their body cannot be verified — goto-instrument crashes under --enforce-contract
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: fb9c9a17-02a2-4e4c-870b-ee03d8bf9757
8	---
9	
10	`ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`, `ZopfliLengthsToSymbols` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — body mallocs `bl_count`/`next_code`), and any `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` function whose **body calls `malloc`/`free`/`realloc` directly** cannot be verified by `run-cbmc`. goto-instrument aborts BEFORE cbmc runs, with:
11	
12	```
13	instrument_spec_assigns.cpp:597 function: create_car_expr
14	Condition: size.has_value()
15	Reason: no definite size for lvalue target: malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail  (type bool, <builtin-library-malloc>)
16	```
17	
18	Root cause (proven by elimination, not clause-specific): `run-cbmc` always does `goto-instrument --enforce-contract <fn>` plus `--add-library` (which links CBMC's malloc model). When the function body calls malloc, the model's internal `should_malloc_fail` bool write gets caught by the frame-condition / car (conditional-address-range) instrumentation, which can't size it → invariant violation → `Aborted`. It is INDEPENDENT of contract content:
19	- removed caller `assigns` → crash; removed ALL `assigns` in file → crash
20	- callee memory-predicates (`is_fresh`→`rw_ok`/`1`) neutralized → crash
21	- callees given trivial `requires(1)/ensures(1)` → crash
22	- caller `is_fresh` requires removed → crash
23	
24	So NO specification on the function can avoid it, and you can't remove the malloc calls (no C edits). goto-cc still ACCEPTS the contract (compile passes); the abort is purely in the goto-instrument enforce step. Contrast: the documented is_fresh/depth-200 vacuity wall ([[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]]) hits functions that take is_fresh pointer params but DON'T call malloc themselves — those at least reach cbmc.
25	
26	Deliverable for `ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`: left a sound, strong contract anyway (requires `n>=0`, `1<=maxbits<=15`, is_fresh on `frequencies`/`bitlengths` of `n` elems; assigns object_whole(bitlengths); ensures return ∈{0,1} and `forall k<n: bitlengths[k] <= maxbits`). It is correct/sound but the tool can't check it. Callees `InitLists/BoundaryPM/BoundaryPMFinal/ExtractBitLengths` already have contracts. See [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]] (always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc`) and [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]].
27

file.content:
---
name: cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash
description: zopfli.c functions that call malloc() directly in their body cannot be verified — goto-instrument crashes under --enforce-contract
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: fb9c9a17-02a2-4e4c-870b-ee03d8bf9757
---

`ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`, `ZopfliLengthsToSymbols` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — body mallocs `bl_count`/`next_code`), and any `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` function whose **body calls `malloc`/`free`/`realloc` directly** cannot be verified by `run-cbmc`. goto-instrument aborts BEFORE cbmc runs, with:

```
instrument_spec_assigns.cpp:597 function: create_car_expr
Condition: size.has_value()
Reason: no definite size for lvalue target: malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail  (type bool, <builtin-library-malloc>)
```

Root cause (proven by elimination, not clause-specific): `run-cbmc` always does `goto-instrument --enforce-contract <fn>` plus `--add-library` (which links CBMC's malloc model). When the function body calls malloc, the model's internal `should_malloc_fail` bool write gets caught by the frame-condition / car (conditional-address-range) instrumentation, which can't size it → invariant violation → `Aborted`. It is INDEPENDENT of contract content:
- removed caller `assigns` → crash; removed ALL `assigns` in file → crash
- callee memory-predicates (`is_fresh`→`rw_ok`/`1`) neutralized → crash
- callees given trivial `requires(1)/ensures(1)` → crash
- caller `is_fresh` requires removed → crash

So NO specification on the function can avoid it, and you can't remove the malloc calls (no C edits). goto-cc still ACCEPTS the contract (compile passes); the abort is purely in the goto-instrument enforce step. Contrast: the documented is_fresh/depth-200 vacuity wall ([[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]]) hits functions that take is_fresh pointer params but DON'T call malloc themselves — those at least reach cbmc.

Deliverable for `ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`: left a sound, strong contract anyway (requires `n>=0`, `1<=maxbits<=15`, is_fresh on `frequencies`/`bitlengths` of `n` elems; assigns object_whole(bitlengths); ensures return ∈{0,1} and `forall k<n: bitlengths[k] <= maxbits`). It is correct/sound but the tool can't check it. Callees `InitLists/BoundaryPM/BoundaryPMFinal/ExtractBitLengths` already have contracts. See [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]] (always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc`) and [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]].

content.text: Let me read the EncodeTree function and check the other memory files.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse
3	description: read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashes the mutation-testing tree-sitter parser for all functions in zopfli.c
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
8	---
9	
10	In `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c`, `read_stdin_to_bytes` has a `while (...) __CPROVER_assigns(...) __CPROVER_loop_invariant(...) { ... }` loop contract (around line 3553). Tree-sitter's C grammar mis-parses the `__CPROVER_loop_invariant(...)` annotation immediately before the `{` as a `function_definition` with a `parenthesized_declarator`. This makes `tools/util/tree_sitter_utils.py:_get_function_definition_name` hit `AssertionError: Unexpected declarator shape: parenthesized_declarator`.
11	
12	Consequence: `generate_mutants_and_compute_score` (the mutation-testing step of `run-cbmc`) parses the WHOLE file and crashes — so it crashes for EVERY function in zopfli.c, even though CBMC verification itself (goto-cc/cbmc) succeeds first. The crash happens only AFTER `is_function_verified` is True, so the function did verify.
13	
14	Function-level contracts parse fine; only loop-level (`__CPROVER_loop_invariant`) annotations trigger it, and no reformat avoids it.
15	
16	Workaround to still get a mutation score for some OTHER function F: copy zopfli.c to a scratch file, strip the `__CPROVER_assigns(ch,...)` and `__CPROVER_loop_invariant(...)` lines (only read_stdin has them), and run `run-cbmc --function F` on the copy. Loop contracts aren't applied during F's verification anyway (only `--enforce-contract F`), so F's result/mutants are unaffected. See [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]].
17

file.content:
---
name: cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse
description: read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashes the mutation-testing tree-sitter parser for all functions in zopfli.c
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
---

In `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c`, `read_stdin_to_bytes` has a `while (...) __CPROVER_assigns(...) __CPROVER_loop_invariant(...) { ... }` loop contract (around line 3553). Tree-sitter's C grammar mis-parses the `__CPROVER_loop_invariant(...)` annotation immediately before the `{` as a `function_definition` with a `parenthesized_declarator`. This makes `tools/util/tree_sitter_utils.py:_get_function_definition_name` hit `AssertionError: Unexpected declarator shape: parenthesized_declarator`.

Consequence: `generate_mutants_and_compute_score` (the mutation-testing step of `run-cbmc`) parses the WHOLE file and crashes — so it crashes for EVERY function in zopfli.c, even though CBMC verification itself (goto-cc/cbmc) succeeds first. The crash happens only AFTER `is_function_verified` is True, so the function did verify.

Function-level contracts parse fine; only loop-level (`__CPROVER_loop_invariant`) annotations trigger it, and no reformat avoids it.

Workaround to still get a mutation score for some OTHER function F: copy zopfli.c to a scratch file, strip the `__CPROVER_assigns(ch,...)` and `__CPROVER_loop_invariant(...)` lines (only read_stdin has them), and run `run-cbmc --function F` on the copy. Loop contracts aren't applied during F's verification anyway (only `--enforce-contract F`), so F's result/mutants are unaffected. See [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]].

content.content:
1	---
2	name: cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity
3	description: Why functions with several is_fresh pointer params verify vacuously (0 mutation kills) under run-cbmc
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
8	---
9	
10	`run-cbmc` runs CBMC with a fixed `--depth 200` (`_CBMC_DEPTH` in `tools/run_cbmc_and_mutation_testing.py`, not env-configurable; do not hard-code depth in specs).
11	
12	In `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` (which `#include`s `<stdlib.h>`), each `__CPROVER_is_fresh(...)` in a `requires` clause routes through CBMC's full `malloc` model (memory-map disjointness, leak tracking, should-fail check) — ~70 GOTO steps per fresh object. A function needing valid memory for ~4 pointer params (e.g. `AddBit(int bit, unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)`) builds a harness of ~280–300 steps, which EXCEEDS depth 200 before the body runs.
13	
14	Effect: CBMC truncates the path, the postconditions (checked at function exit) are never reached, so they pass vacuously. The tool reports "verified successfully" but it is a vacuous proof — and mutation testing then kills 0 mutants regardless of how strong the spec is. Symptom to recognize: adding `__CPROVER_ensures(1 == 0)` still "verifies" (run via direct cbmc and grep `postcondition.*SUCCESS`; raise `--depth` to ~350 to see it flip to FAILURE).
15	
16	Without `<stdlib.h>` the same 4-is_fresh harness fits in depth 200 (light built-in alloc) — so it's the malloc model, not is_fresh per se. `rw_ok` for scalars is lighter but unsound for written-through out-params. No sound formulation fits depth 200 here.
17	
18	Confirmed instances: `AddBit`, `BoundaryPMFinal`, `BoundaryPM`, and `ExtractBitLengths` (recursive; params `Node*(*lists)[2], leaves, pool` — its self-call + `InitNode` are both replaced by contracts, so the contract must be inductive). For `BoundaryPM` a requires(is_fresh pool/lists/leaves)+assigns(object_whole(lists),pool->next) contract verifies at depth 200 but is vacuous (ensures(1==0) SUCCEEDS at 200, FAILS at 400). Extra obstacle: any `__CPROVER_ensures` that dereferences a nested list pointer (e.g. `__CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count)`) fails NON-vacuously — the entry-time snapshot derefs the nondeterministic `lists[i][j]` (R_OK/bounds FAILURE at the body's first `lists[index][1]->count`). Only scalar facts like `pool->next >= __CPROVER_old(pool->next)` are safely addable. Net: kill score 0/27 is tooling-forced, not spec weakness. `BoundaryPMFinal` (same `Node*(*lists)[2],leaves,pool` shape, plus nested `is_fresh` on `lists[index][1]`, `lists[index-1][0/1]`, `pool->next` so even heavier harness) verifies a STRONG branch-conditional `ensures` (deref nested list pointers — safe here because those nested pointers are made fresh in requires), but `ensures(0==1)` still SUCCEEDS at depth 200 AND 450 → vacuous, 0/16 kills forced.
19	
20	`ExtractBitLengths(Node *chain, Node *leaves, unsigned *bitlengths)` (3 indexed pointer params; restricted to single-node chain via `requires chain->tail==0` so the `chain->tail` walk is safe without per-node freshness) directly demonstrates the wall both ways: (1) `is_fresh` on all 3 → "verified" but VACUOUS (0/12 kills, loop-body mutants at lines 332-340 all survive). (2) Swapping the two *indexed* pointers (`leaves`, `bitlengths`) to the light `r_ok`/`w_ok` predicates DOES fit depth 200 and REACHES the body (non-vacuous) — but then CBMC cannot discharge the array bounds / `assigns`-assignable checks: `r_ok`/`w_ok`, even combined with `__CPROVER_OBJECT_SIZE(p)==n` and `__CPROVER_POINTER_OFFSET(p)==0` assumptions, do NOT make the bounds checker treat the object as sized — `bitlengths[leaves[k].count]` reports "pointer outside object bounds" + "is assignable: FAILURE". Only `is_fresh` establishes a bounded object for write-through/indexed accesses, and only `is_fresh` is heavy enough to overrun depth 200. So: indexed pointers MUST be `is_fresh` (sound) → vacuous; or light predicates (fits) → unsound bounds FAILURE. No middle ground. Net 0/12 is tooling-forced.
21	
22	Bonus: at depth >=350, CBMC finds a real latent bug — `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` (macro in zopfli.h) writes through `realloc`'s return with no NULL check. See [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]] and [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]].
23

file.content:
---
name: cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity
description: Why functions with several is_fresh pointer params verify vacuously (0 mutation kills) under run-cbmc
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
---

`run-cbmc` runs CBMC with a fixed `--depth 200` (`_CBMC_DEPTH` in `tools/run_cbmc_and_mutation_testing.py`, not env-configurable; do not hard-code depth in specs).

In `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` (which `#include`s `<stdlib.h>`), each `__CPROVER_is_fresh(...)` in a `requires` clause routes through CBMC's full `malloc` model (memory-map disjointness, leak tracking, should-fail check) — ~70 GOTO steps per fresh object. A function needing valid memory for ~4 pointer params (e.g. `AddBit(int bit, unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)`) builds a harness of ~280–300 steps, which EXCEEDS depth 200 before the body runs.

Effect: CBMC truncates the path, the postconditions (checked at function exit) are never reached, so they pass vacuously. The tool reports "verified successfully" but it is a vacuous proof — and mutation testing then kills 0 mutants regardless of how strong the spec is. Symptom to recognize: adding `__CPROVER_ensures(1 == 0)` still "verifies" (run via direct cbmc and grep `postcondition.*SUCCESS`; raise `--depth` to ~350 to see it flip to FAILURE).

Without `<stdlib.h>` the same 4-is_fresh harness fits in depth 200 (light built-in alloc) — so it's the malloc model, not is_fresh per se. `rw_ok` for scalars is lighter but unsound for written-through out-params. No sound formulation fits depth 200 here.

Confirmed instances: `AddBit`, `BoundaryPMFinal`, `BoundaryPM`, and `ExtractBitLengths` (recursive; params `Node*(*lists)[2], leaves, pool` — its self-call + `InitNode` are both replaced by contracts, so the contract must be inductive). For `BoundaryPM` a requires(is_fresh pool/lists/leaves)+assigns(object_whole(lists),pool->next) contract verifies at depth 200 but is vacuous (ensures(1==0) SUCCEEDS at 200, FAILS at 400). Extra obstacle: any `__CPROVER_ensures` that dereferences a nested list pointer (e.g. `__CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count)`) fails NON-vacuously — the entry-time snapshot derefs the nondeterministic `lists[i][j]` (R_OK/bounds FAILURE at the body's first `lists[index][1]->count`). Only scalar facts like `pool->next >= __CPROVER_old(pool->next)` are safely addable. Net: kill score 0/27 is tooling-forced, not spec weakness. `BoundaryPMFinal` (same `Node*(*lists)[2],leaves,pool` shape, plus nested `is_fresh` on `lists[index][1]`, `lists[index-1][0/1]`, `pool->next` so even heavier harness) verifies a STRONG branch-conditional `ensures` (deref nested list pointers — safe here because those nested pointers are made fresh in requires), but `ensures(0==1)` still SUCCEEDS at depth 200 AND 450 → vacuous, 0/16 kills forced.

`ExtractBitLengths(Node *chain, Node *leaves, unsigned *bitlengths)` (3 indexed pointer params; restricted to single-node chain via `requires chain->tail==0` so the `chain->tail` walk is safe without per-node freshness) directly demonstrates the wall both ways: (1) `is_fresh` on all 3 → "verified" but VACUOUS (0/12 kills, loop-body mutants at lines 332-340 all survive). (2) Swapping the two *indexed* pointers (`leaves`, `bitlengths`) to the light `r_ok`/`w_ok` predicates DOES fit depth 200 and REACHES the body (non-vacuous) — but then CBMC cannot discharge the array bounds / `assigns`-assignable checks: `r_ok`/`w_ok`, even combined with `__CPROVER_OBJECT_SIZE(p)==n` and `__CPROVER_POINTER_OFFSET(p)==0` assumptions, do NOT make the bounds checker treat the object as sized — `bitlengths[leaves[k].count]` reports "pointer outside object bounds" + "is assignable: FAILURE". Only `is_fresh` establishes a bounded object for write-through/indexed accesses, and only `is_fresh` is heavy enough to overrun depth 200. So: indexed pointers MUST be `is_fresh` (sound) → vacuous; or light predicates (fits) → unsound bounds FAILURE. No middle ground. Net 0/12 is tooling-forced.

Bonus: at depth >=350, CBMC finds a real latent bug — `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` (macro in zopfli.h) writes through `realloc`'s return with no NULL check. See [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]] and [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]].

1045	deflate spec to have 0 distance codes. On top of that, some mobile phones
1046	require at least two distance codes. To support these decoders too (but
1047	potentially at the cost of a few bytes), add dummy code lengths of 1.
1048	References to this bug can be found in the changelog of

file.content:
size that you consider the array to be, not the internal allocation size.
Precondition: allocated size of data is at least a power of two greater than or
equal than *size.
*/
#define ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(/* T */ value, /* T** */ data, /* size_t* */ size)        \
    {                                                                                \
        if (!((*size) & ((*size) - 1)))                                              \
        {                                                                            \
            /*double alloc size if it's a power of two*/                             \
            (*data) = (*size) == 0 ? malloc(sizeof(**data))                          \
                                   : realloc((*data), (*size) * 2 * sizeof(**data)); \
            memset((*data) + (*size), 0, (*size) * sizeof(**data));                  \
        }                                                                            \
        (*data)[(*size)] = (value);                                                  \
        (*size)++;                                                                   \
    }

/*
Outputs minimum-redundancy length-limited code bitlengths for symbols with the
given counts. The bitlengths are limited by maxbits.

content.text: Confirmed: EncodeTree's body directly invokes `malloc`/`realloc`/`memset` (via `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA`) and `free`. Per my prior finding [[cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash]], this crashes goto-instrument under `--enforce-contract`. Let me check the array-size constants and callee contracts so I can still write a sound, strong spec, and confirm the crash for this specific function.

input.command: cd /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code; for f in ZopfliCalculateBitLengths AddBits AddHuffmanBits; do echo "=== $f ==="; grep -n "^[a-z].*$f(\|^static.* $f(\| $f(" zopfli.c | head -3; done

content.content:
665	void ZopfliCalculateBitLengths(const size_t *count, size_t n, int maxbits,
666	                               unsigned *bitlengths)
667	// Thin wrapper that asserts the length-limited code length computation cannot
668	// fail. `count` and `bitlengths` are arrays of `n` symbols and `maxbits` is the
669	// inclusive DEFLATE code-length limit. The preconditions are exactly the inputs
670	// that make ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths return 0, so the internal
671	// `assert(!error)` always holds.
672	__CPROVER_requires(n <= (size_t)INT_MAX)
673	__CPROVER_requires(maxbits >= 1 && maxbits <= 15)
674	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(count, n * sizeof(*count)))
675	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bitlengths, n * sizeof(*bitlengths)))
676	// Enough code-length room for every symbol, and 9 free bits per frequency.
677	__CPROVER_requires(n <= ((size_t)1 << maxbits))
678	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
679	    size_t k;
680	    (k < n) ==> count[k] < ((size_t)1 << (sizeof(*count) * CHAR_BIT - 9))
681	})
682	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(bitlengths))
683	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
684	    size_t k;
685	    (k < n) ==> (bitlengths[k] <= (unsigned)maxbits)
686	})
687	{
688	    int error = ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths(count, n, maxbits, bitlengths);
689	    (void)error;
690	    assert(!error);
691	}
692	
693	/*
694	Adds bits, like AddBits, but the order is inverted. The deflate specification
695	uses both orders in one standard.
696	*/
697	static void AddHuffmanBits(unsigned symbol, unsigned length,
698	                           unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out,
699	                           size_t *outsize)
700	// Writes the low `length` bits of `symbol`, MSB first, to the bitstream.
701	// bp points to the current in-byte bit position (0..7); out/outsize describe
702	// the dynamic output array whose backing buffer holds at least *outsize bytes
703	// (the invariant maintained by ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA).
704	// length is bounded by the integer width so that `symbol >> i` is well defined.
705	__CPROVER_requires(length <= 32)
706	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bp, sizeof(*bp)))
707	__CPROVER_requires(*bp <= 7)
708	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
709	__CPROVER_requires(*outsize >= 1 && *outsize <= __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / 2)
710	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
711	// The backing buffer holds at least *outsize+1 bytes, so the last byte (index
712	// *outsize-1) is always present and there is room for one appended byte.
713	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, *outsize + 1))
714	__CPROVER_assigns(*bp, *outsize, *out, __CPROVER_object_whole(*out))
715	// Each iteration advances bp by one modulo 8, so after `length` bits the bit
716	// position is the old position plus length, modulo 8.
717	__CPROVER_ensures(*bp == ((__CPROVER_old(*bp) + length) & 7))
718	// A fresh byte is appended exactly on the iterations where bp is 0, i.e. once
719	// per 8 bits written, starting immediately if the old position was 0.
720	__CPROVER_ensures(
721	    *outsize == __CPROVER_old(*outsize) +
722	        (length == 0 ? 0
723	                     : (((__CPROVER_old(*bp) + length - 1) / 8) +
724	                        (__CPROVER_old(*bp) == 0 ? 1 : 0))))
725	{
726	    /* TODO(lode): make more efficient (add more bits at once). */
727	    unsigned i;
728	    for (i = 0; i < length; i++)
729	    {
730	        unsigned bit = (symbol >> (length - i - 1)) & 1;
731	        if (*bp == 0)
732	            ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(0, out, outsize);
733	        (*out)[*outsize - 1] |= bit << *bp;
734	        *bp = (*bp + 1) & 7;
735	    }
736	}
737	
738	static void AddBits(unsigned symbol, unsigned length,
739	                    unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)

content.text:
I have enough context. EncodeTree's body invokes `malloc`/`realloc`/`memset` (via `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA`) and `free` directly — exactly the pattern my prior finding [[cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash]] documents as causing goto-instrument to abort under `--enforce-contract`, independent of contract content. I'll write a sound, strong contract anyway and confirm whether the crash reproduces for this specific function.

Let me write the spec, focusing on the cleanly-specifiable size-only mode (the path used by `CalculateTreeSize`).

content.content: The file /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: int use_16, int use_17, int use_18,
+// Computes the DEFLATE encoding size of the litlen/dist code-length trees.
+// This contract specifies the "size only" mode (out == NULL), the path used by
+// CalculateTreeSize: no bits are emitted and no parameter is written.
+// ll_lengths/d_lengths are the full DEFLATE code-length tables (ZOPFLI_NUM_LL /
+// ZOPFLI_NUM_D entries). Each code length is a valid DEFLATE length (<= 15),
+// which keeps every clcounts[symbol]/clcl[symbol] index in 0..18 in bounds.
+// Size-only mode writes through no parameter; only local scratch is touched.
unsigned lld_total; /* Total amount of literal, length, distance codes. */
/* Runlength encoded version of lengths of litlen and dist trees. */

content.text: We reached the mutation-testing step (`generate_mutants_and_compute_score`) — which per [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]] only happens *after* CBMC verification succeeds. So EncodeTree actually verified (it did not hit the goto-instrument malloc crash). The crash here is the known tree-sitter misparse of `read_stdin_to_bytes`'s loop contract, affecting all functions in the file. Let me apply the documented workaround to confirm verification and get a real mutation score.

content.text: Let me create a scratch copy with the loop-contract lines stripped (the workaround) and run on it.

input.command:
cp zopfli.c /tmp/et_scratch.c
# strip only the loop-contract annotation lines that confuse tree-sitter (not function contracts)
sed -i '/__CPROVER_loop_invariant/d; /__CPROVER_assigns(ch,/d' /tmp/et_scratch.c
grep -c "__CPROVER_loop_invariant" /tmp/et_scratch.c
run-cbmc --function EncodeTree --file /tmp/et_scratch.c -I /tmp/cbmc-inc 2>&1 | tail -30
